description: Migration from v5 vault integration to v6 save payment session

v6_pattern.token_usage: Server-side order creation with vault token
v6_pattern.security_model: Server-side token management with user authentication

without_purchase.name: Save payment method without initial purchase
without_purchase.use_case: Add payment method to account without immediate purchase

with_purchase.name: Save payment method during checkout
with_purchase.use_case: One-click checkout - save payment during first purchase

onApprove.return: void - NO Promise return (different from one-time payments)

onApprove_return_type.reason: Save payment approval doesn't require immediate server action

orderId_parameter.save_payment: orderId is FORBIDDEN - will cause error
orderId_parameter.reason: Save payment doesn't create orders initially

vaultSetupToken.usage: Send to server to create payment token

create_setup_token.description: Create vault setup token for payment method approval

create_payment_token.description: Create permanent payment token from setup token
security: Validate vaultSetupToken is from current authenticated user
Never expose token to unauthorized users

create_order_with_vault.description: Create order using saved payment token

delete_payment_token.authentication: Require user authentication and token ownership validation

security_requirements: NEVER store payment tokens in localStorage or sessionStorage
ALWAYS store tokens server-side with encryption
ALWAYS associate tokens with authenticated users only
ALWAYS validate token ownership before usage
ALWAYS implement proper user authentication for vault access
ALWAYS validate setup tokens before creating payment tokens
ALWAYS log vault operations for audit trails
ALWAYS implement customer consent for payment method saving
ALWAYS validate vault permissions in PayPal Developer Dashboard

upgrade_steps.action: Enable vault permissions in PayPal Developer Dashboard
upgrade_steps.location: Dashboard → My Apps & Credentials → App → Features

upgrade_steps.action: Setup webhook for vault token events

eligibility_check.usage: const methods = await sdkInstance.findEligibleMethods({ currencyCode: 'USD', paymentFlow: 'VAULT_WITHOUT_PAYMENT' });

webhook_integration.description: Triggered when payment token is created during checkout
webhook_integration.use_case: Save payment method with purchase flow

VAULT_NOT_ENABLED.description: Vault permissions not enabled in PayPal Dashboard
VAULT_NOT_ENABLED.user_message: Payment saving is not available for your account.
VAULT_NOT_ENABLED.resolution: Enable vault in PayPal Developer Dashboard

SETUP_TOKEN_EXPIRED.description: Setup token expired before creating payment token
SETUP_TOKEN_EXPIRED.user_message: Setup session expired. Please try again.
SETUP_TOKEN_EXPIRED.resolution: Create payment token promptly after approval

CUSTOMER_NOT_ELIGIBLE.description: Customer not eligible for vault functionality
CUSTOMER_NOT_ELIGIBLE.user_message: Your account is not eligible for saving payment methods.

TOKEN_ALREADY_EXISTS.user_message: This payment method is already saved.
TOKEN_ALREADY_EXISTS.resolution: Check for existing tokens before creating

best_practices: Always validate vault permissions before showing save option
Implement customer consent clearly and transparently
Provide easy access to manage saved payment methods
Allow customers to delete saved methods anytime
Never expose payment token details to frontend
Encrypt all payment tokens at rest
Log all vault operations for compliance
Implement token expiration and cleanup processes
Validate token ownership on every usage
Test vault flows thoroughly in sandbox
Provide clear UI feedback during save process
breaking_changes: Payment tokens MUST be stored server-side (v5: client-side allowed)
onApprove does NOT return Promise (v5: could return Promise)
orderId parameter is FORBIDDEN (v5: could be used)
Requires user authentication for token access (v5: optional)
Setup tokens required for save without purchase (v5: direct tokenization)
Webhooks recommended for save with purchase (v5: optional)
